summary: An interactive form that submits user input to a URL.
use: With action and method; GET for searches and filters (shareable URLs), POST for changes.
avoid: Don't nest forms, and don't hijack submission with JS before the native behavior works.
attributes: autocomplete — on/off default for the form
snippet:
<form action='/search' method='get'>
  <label for='q'>Search</label>
  <input type='search' id='q' name='q'>
  <button type='submit'>Go</button>
</form>

summary: The caption of a form control, programmatically bound to it.
use: Every control gets one — via for/id or by wrapping the control; clicking the label focuses the control.
avoid: placeholder is not a label: it vanishes on input and fails contrast; title is not a label either.
attributes: for — the id of the labeled control

summary: The shape-shifting form control: 22 types from text to color to file.
use: Pick the specific type (email, url, tel, date, number, range, search…) — each brings validation, the right mobile keyboard, and the right widget; add autocomplete tokens.
avoid: Don't use type='text' for typed data, and don't suppress native validation only to rebuild it worse in JS.
attributes: required / min / max / step / pattern / maxlength — constraints
snippet:
<label for='qty'>Quantity</label>
<input type='number' id='qty' name='qty' min='1' max='10' value='1' required>

summary: A button — focusable, keyboard-activatable, screen-reader-announced, for free.
use: Always with an explicit type: submit, reset, or button; also the invoker for popovers via popovertarget and commands via commandfor.
avoid: Never a div or a with onclick pretending to be a button; never omit type inside forms (default is submit).
attributes: form — associate with a form by id
snippet: A popover, no JavaScript involved.

summary: A dropdown of options, natively keyboard- and screen-reader-friendly.
use: For choosing one (or with multiple, several) of a closed set; group related options with optgroup.
avoid: Never rebuild it as a div listbox — you lose typeahead, mobile pickers, and AT support; style it with the customizable-select CSS instead.
snippet:
<label for='edition'>Edition</label>
<select id='edition' name='edition'>
  <optgroup label='Available now'>
    <option value='cloth' selected>Clothbound</option>
    <option value='paper'>Paperback</option>
  </optgroup>
</select>

summary: Mirrors the chosen option's content into a customizable select's closed button.
use: Inside a button that is the first child of select, activated by CSS appearance: base-select — enables rich styled selects with native behavior.
avoid: Experimental and Chromium-first; frameworks re-rendering option content won't auto-update the mirrored clone.

summary: Native autocomplete suggestions for an input — suggestions, not restrictions.
use: Link it from an input's list attribute; users can pick a suggestion or type anything else.
avoid: If the value must come from the set, that's a select, not a datalist.
snippet:
<label for='topic'>Favorite element</label>
<input type='text' id='topic' name='topic' list='elements'>
<datalist id='elements'>
  <option value='hgroup'></option>
  <option value='output'></option>
</datalist>

summary: Groups related options under a label inside a select.
use: When the option list has natural categories — the label renders as a non-selectable group heading.
avoid: Only valid inside select; don't nest optgroups.

summary: One choice inside a select or datalist.
use: With value when the submitted value differs from the visible text; selected preselects.
avoid: Keep option content simple; rich content belongs to the customizable-select pattern.

use: With a label, rows/cols as a size hint, and maxlength when limits exist; field-sizing: content in CSS auto-grows it.
avoid: Its initial value is its text content — mind stray whitespace between the tags.

summary: The result of a calculation or user action, with built-in live-region behavior.
use: For computed totals, scores, conversion results; its for attribute lists the inputs it derives from, and screen readers announce updates.
avoid: Not for static prose — it means 'this value was produced by the page'.
attributes: for — space-separated ids of contributing inputs

summary: Groups related controls, with legend as the group's caption.
use: Mandatory for radio and checkbox groups — the legend is announced with each control's label; disabled on it disables the whole group.
avoid: Don't use it as a generic bordered box; it announces itself as a group.
snippet:
<fieldset>
  <legend>Preferred edition</legend>
  <input type='radio' id='ed-text' name='edition' checked>
  <label for='ed-text'>Plain text</label>
  <input type='radio' id='ed-html' name='edition'>
  <label for='ed-html'>Semantic HTML</label>
</fieldset>

summary: The caption of a fieldset, announced with every control inside the group.
use: First child of fieldset, naming the group ('Delivery details', 'Payment method').
avoid: Only valid as fieldset's first child; keep it short — screen readers repeat it per control.

summary: Task completion: how much of a known process is done.
use: With max and value for determinate progress; omit value for indeterminate 'working…'.
avoid: For a measurement within a range (disk usage, score) use meter — progress means a task.
snippet:
<label for='done'>Guide completion</label>
<progress id='done' max='9' value='8'>8 of 9 sections</progress>

summary: A scalar measurement within a known range: stock, disk usage, password strength, a score.
use: With min/max plus low/high/optimum so the browser can color-code good, warning, and critical zones.
avoid: For task completion use progress — meter is a gauge, not a journey.
snippet:
<label for='stock'>Stock</label>
<meter id='stock' min='0' max='500' low='50' high='400' optimum='500' value='132'>132 of 500</meter>
